channel.title: Captain Dadders — Flight Expeditions
channel.description: Following the Lewis & Clark Corps of Discovery route by air — MSFS 2024 expedition blog.

item.title: Leg 4: River Highway to Cincinnati — I41 to KLUK
item.description: Following the Ohio River's busy highway to Cincinnati — the largest city on the route since Pittsburgh. Lewis spent a week here in 1803 writing to Jefferson, coordinating with Clark, and gathering natural history specimens. First paved landing of the expedition.

item.title: Leg 3: Above the Morning Fog — 75D to I41
item.description: Fog off the Ohio River forced a climb to 4,000 feet — the same phenomenon Lewis carefully measured and recorded in his journal. Power plants, farmland, and a ground-loop landing at Robert Newlon Field near Huntington, WV.

item.title: Leg 2: Wheeling and the Mound — 1G8 to 75D
item.description: A short hop along the Ohio River past Wheeling, West Virginia and the ancient Grave Creek Mound at Moundsville — one of the largest prehistoric burial mounds in North America, and a landmark Lewis would have passed on the river in 1803.

item.title: Leg 1: Departing Pittsburgh — 31D to 1G8
item.description: The expedition gets underway. Following the Ohio River from Inter County Airport past The Point in Pittsburgh to Eddie Dew Memorial Airpark in Toronto, Ohio — the same ground Lewis and Clark covered August 31 to September 5, 1803.
